column1: HABIT 2: Feel hunger 30-60 minutes before eating

column8: 1. Track how long you're hungry before each meal

What does hunger feel like? Hunger feels like an “empty hollow sensation” in your stomach. This may or may not be followed by a “growling stomach”. It may come and go in waves, but over time it tends to build until you eat. It is important not to confuse hunger with a craving or desire to eat. We must learn to decipher between what is hunger and what is a craving.

column1: If you have a snack let me know why in the box.
Each time you miss a meal, put an "N" for "no" in the column.
column8: When do I want to feel it? How much hunger are we talking about? If our goal is to lose weight, then we want to experience/feel hunger for 30-60 minutes before each meal. This amount of time is an indicator that we are in enough of a calorie deficit to lose weight gradually but consistently.

column1: If there are notes you'd like to document, please leave them in the "Notes."

column8: 2. Eat more slowly at each meal
-Put your fork/food down after each bite, don't pick it back up until you have swallowed your food

column1: 2. Eat 3-4 meals each day, no snacking
column8: -Be aware of how fast you're chewing and slow down
